mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in RSAFS signals strengthening household demand that raises near-term revenue expectations for listed firms; higher RSAFS momentum drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest snapshot value was 483895 as of one month before 2017-10-15
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS momentum is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of walk-forward observations or has Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS rises through 1.0% during an ongoing tightening cycle, equity discount rates increase faster than near-term earnings expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 1.0% and its 6-month change is positive
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest snapshot value was 1.15 as of one month before 2017-10-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered FEDFUNDS crossings are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the triggered mean return exceeds the unconditional mean return.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE is below 4.5% and PAYEMS is still rising over 3 months, the labor market supports income growth without signaling recession, so the joint labor signal drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest snapshot value was 4.2 as of one month before 2017-10-15
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with continuing job creation
failure_condition: Reject if the joint UNRATE and PAYEMS trigger produces 20-day forward returns below the unconditional sample median in more than 55% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rising 6-month trend in INDPRO indicates improving industrial output and operating leverage, increasing expected earnings for cyclical firms and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest snapshot value was 104.739 as of two months before 2017-10-15
failure_condition: Reject if positive 6-month INDPRO momentum has a non-positive coefficient in walk-forward predictive regressions for 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation momentum exceeds the level of FEDFUNDS, markets price tighter future policy and lower equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds FEDFUNDS by more than 1 percentage point
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest snapshot value was 246.373 as of one month before 2017-10-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS interactions are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if downside hit rate is not above 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST below 1200 signals weakening residential investment and durable-goods demand, reducing cyclical earnings expectations and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below 1200 and its 6-month change is negative
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest snapshot value was 1180 as of two months before 2017-10-15
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST-below-1200 triggers have average 60-day S&P 500 returns greater than the unconditional average or if the negative signal hit rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 is expanding, UNRATE is below 4.5%, and FEDFUNDS is rising, the expansion is late-cycle and policy tightening compresses valuation multiples, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 year-over-year change > 0, UNRATE < 4.5%, and FEDFUNDS 6-month change > 0.25 percentage point
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 latest snapshot value was 17031.1 as of six months before 2017-10-15
failure_condition: Reject if this three-variable regime produces positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered observations or if its average return is not below the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp PAYEMS slowdown below 100 thousand new jobs in one month signals an abrupt deterioration in labor demand, causing investors to lower near-term growth expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest snapshot value was 146659 as of one month before 2017-10-15
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS slowdown triggers are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or if the triggered downside hit rate is statistically indistinguishable from 50%.
